sentiment_score_definition: x <= -0.35: Bearish; -0.35 < x <= -0.15: Somewhat-Bearish; -0.15 < x < 0.15: Neutral; 0.15 <= x < 0.35: Somewhat_Bullish; x >= 0.35: Bullish
relevance_score_definition: 0 < x <= 1, with a higher score indicating higher relevance.

feed.title: Invitation Homes stock hits 52-week low at $25.28
feed.summary: Invitation Homes Inc. (INVH) stock has fallen to a 52-week low of $25.28, representing a 17.24% decline over the past year. Despite recording positive Q4 2025 earnings which exceeded analyst expectations, the stock experienced a downturn. Analyst firms KeyBanc and Citizens have both revised down their price targets for Invitation Homes, citing market underperformance and oversupply pressures in the real estate sector.

feed.title: Beware the business school case study: the cautionary tale of Southwest Airlines
feed.summary: Business school case studies often present a biased view of success, focusing on companies approved cases and overlooking their eventual decline. The author argues that like Dell and Nokia, Southwest Airlines, once an exemplar, now faces challenges and a departure from its original values, exemplified by its recent Super Bowl ad mocking its historical differentiator. The article suggests that relying solely on past case studies without understanding the transient nature of competitive advantage can be misleading.

feed.title: Wells Fargo Shakes Claim It Fired Workers Who Backed Union
feed.summary: The National Labor Relations Board dismissed an unfair labor practice claim against Wells Fargo & Co. The claim was brought by nonbranch-based employees who alleged that the bank fired members of their proposed bargaining unit before a successful unionization vote in December 2024. Wells Fargo is noted as the first major US bank with unionized employees, with 30 branches having voted to unionize so far.

feed.title: Copart Inc (NASDAQ:CPRT) Lags Estimates
feed.summary: Copart Inc (NASDAQ:CPRT) reported earnings and revenue below Wall Street estimates for the quarter ending January 31, 2026, leading to a 4.46% stock drop. The company's net income fell 9.5% and operating income declined 8.8%, despite recording record U.S. insurance average selling prices. This underperformance was attributed to softer claims activity and lower global insurance unit volumes.

feed.title: Newmont Corporation (NYSE:NEM) Forecast Lower Production
feed.summary: Newmont Corporation (NYSE:NEM) saw its stock fall 4.04% despite beating Wall Street's fourth-quarter profit estimates, driven by record gold prices that offset lower production. The company announced a $1.4 billion investment in newly acquired assets and forecast lower gold production for 2026. Newmont also reported decreases in gold, copper, and silver reserves and resources compared to the previous year.

feed.title: Walmart Inc (NASDAQ:WMT) Issues Cautious Outlook
feed.summary: Walmart Inc (NASDAQ:WMT) saw its stock fall after issuing a cautious annual outlook for fiscal year 2026, which disappointed Wall Street despite solid fourth-quarter results. The company reported strong Q4 FY25 performance with adjusted EPS of 74 cents and revenue of $190.7 billion, both beating estimates. However, the projected net sales growth of 3.5%-4.5% and adjusted earnings between US$2.75 and US$2.85 per share for FY26 were below analyst expectations, raising concerns about sustaining growth under the new CEO.

feed.title: Stocks making big moves this week: Xerox, Vulcan Materials, Mercury General, Wendy's, and Wingstop
feed.summary: This article highlights several stocks that experienced significant movement this week. Xerox rose after a joint venture and new sales structure. Conversely, Vulcan Materials and Mercury General both fell due to disappointing earnings and outlooks. Wendy's surged on news of activist investor interest and a potential takeover, while Wingstop saw its stock increase after strong profit beats in its quarterly earnings.

feed.title: Merck vs. Bristol Myers: Which Pharma Stock Is a Better Pick in 2026?
feed.summary: This article compares Merck (MRK) and Bristol Myers Squibb (BMY) as investment options for 2026, analyzing their fundamentals, growth outlooks, risks, and valuations. While Merck boasts strong oncology sales driven by Keytruda and a robust pipeline, it faces headwinds from patent expirations and declining sales of other drugs. Bristol Myers Squibb also has a strong oncology portfolio and is working to mitigate generic competition for legacy drugs through acquisitions and cost savings. Considering valuation, dividend yield, and near-term outlook, Bristol Myers Squibb is presented as the more compelling investment choice.

feed.title: Lam Research Named Top Semiconductor Pick As AI-Driven Manufacturing Supercycle Shifts Into High Gear
feed.summary: Benzinga editor Anusuya Lahiri reports that Vivek Arya of BofA Securities has named Lam Research (NASDAQ: LRCX) as his top semiconductor pick. This is due to a strong, multi-year wafer fab equipment (WFE) upcycle driven by AI and advanced chip demand. Lam Research is poised to benefit from this surge, with a projected industry-wide spending of $135 billion in WFE by 2026 and potential spillover into 2027.

feed.title: Sbrocco, Westinghouse Air Brake EVP, sells $246k in WAB stock
feed.summary: Gregory Sbrocco, EVP of Operations at Westinghouse Air Brake Technologies CORP (NASDAQ:WAB), sold 952 shares of WAB stock for over $246,000. This transaction occurred on February 19, 2026, with the stock trading near its 52-week high after significant gains. Despite the sale, Sbrocco still directly owns 21,159 shares, and the company recently reported strong Q4 2025 financial results, leading to a raised price target from Stephens.

feed.title: PWR: Baird Raises Price Target on Quanta Services to $606 | PWR Stock News
feed.summary: Baird has raised its price target on Quanta Services (PWR) to $606.00 from $490.00, while maintaining an "Outperform" rating. This decision reflects a positive sentiment, aligning with other analysts who have also recently increased their price targets for the company. Despite the positive analyst outlook, GuruFocus estimates suggest a potential downside for PWR based on its GF Value.

feed.title: Blue Owl's Redemption Shift Shakes Private Credit Industry
feed.summary: Blue Owl Capital Inc. has disrupted the private credit market by restricting investor withdrawals from one of its retail-focused funds, leading to a sell-off in its shares and those of other alternative asset managers like Apollo, Blackstone, KKR, and Ares. This move, prompted by rising redemption requests and liquidity pressures, shifts focus from credit performance to structural liquidity risk and suggests a reassessment of profit expectations in the rapidly expanding private credit sector. The market is now prioritizing liquidity management and fund structure as much as yield and credit quality.

feed.title: GLPI “open for business” in New York City
feed.summary: Gaming & Leisure Properties Inc. (GLPI) is actively exploring investment opportunities in New York City's burgeoning casino market, particularly interested in Bally's Bronx and other potential projects, but emphasizes that its loans would require partial ownership or purchase options. Despite Bally's current negative cash flow and stock trading at a discount, GLPI maintains a long-term view, citing ongoing development and confidence in properties like Bally's Chicago and the planned Las Vegas resort. The company also discussed other market dynamics, including Virginia's potential igaming legislation and the Canadian casino market, while asserting that their investment decisions are not solely based on interest rates or a tenant's balance sheet percentage.

feed.title: MGRX (Mangoceuticals, Inc., NASDAQ) up 50.75% intraday 20 Feb 2026: TRT sales drive heavy volume
feed.summary: Mangoceuticals, Inc. (MGRX) experienced a significant 50.75% intraday surge on February 20, 2026, driven by strong initial sales results from its $99 injectable TRT program. The company reported 336% month-over-month sales growth and a 54% reduction in customer acquisition cost, leading to a massive volume spike of nearly 300 million shares. While strong TRT sales propelled the stock, Meyka AI's model projects a 12-month fair value of USD 0.26, indicating potential downside due to ongoing net losses and liquidity risks.

feed.title: Helix Energy Solns Gr Earnings Preview
feed.summary: Helix Energy Solutions Group (NYSE: HLX) is set to release its quarterly earnings on February 23, 2026. Analysts project an earnings per share (EPS) of $0.02, and investors will be looking for the company to surpass estimates and provide positive guidance for the next quarter. The company's stock has shown positive performance, up 13.44% over the last 52 weeks, despite missing EPS estimates by $0.01 in the previous quarter.

feed.title: Targa Resources (NYSE:TRGP) Stock Price Expected to Rise, Wells Fargo & Company Analyst Says
feed.summary: Wells Fargo & Company has raised its price target for Targa Resources (NYSE:TRGP) to $248 from $207, maintaining an "overweight" rating and suggesting a 7.7% upside. This follows Targa's strong Q4 EPS beat, record 2025 results, a dividend increase, and a bullish 2026 outlook. While the stock currently holds a "Moderate Buy" consensus rating among analysts, some institutional investors have recently adjusted their stakes in the company.

feed.title: Occidental Petroleum (NYSE:OXY) Given New $47.00 Price Target at Wells Fargo & Company
feed.summary: Wells Fargo & Company has raised its price target for Occidental Petroleum (NYSE:OXY) from $40.00 to $47.00, though it maintains an "underweight" rating, suggesting an 8.05% potential downside. Despite beating Q4 EPS expectations, Occidental Petroleum missed on revenue but announced an increased quarterly dividend and significant debt reduction. The consensus among analysts remains a "Hold" with an average target price of $48.52, indicating mixed views on the stock's future performance.

feed.title: Benchmark Cuts Hillman Solutions (NASDAQ:HLMN) Price Target to $14.00
feed.summary: Benchmark has lowered its price target for Hillman Solutions (NASDAQ:HLMN) to $14.00 from $15.00 while maintaining a "buy" rating, reflecting a potential 54% upside. This adjustment follows Hillman's recent quarterly earnings report, which met EPS estimates but slightly missed revenue expectations. Despite the target cut, institutional ownership remains very high, with several major funds increasing their stakes in recent quarters.

feed.title: What Does the Market Think About Immunic Inc?
feed.summary: Immunic Inc's (IMUX) short interest has decreased by 7.19% since its last report, now standing at 6.57 million shares sold short, which is 5.55% of its float. This decline suggests a more bullish sentiment among investors compared to the previous report. The company's short interest is also lower than its peer group average of 10.21%.

feed.title: Deep Dive Into Integer Holdings Stock: Analyst Perspectives (6 Ratings)
feed.summary: Integer Holdings (NYSE: ITGR) has been evaluated by 6 analysts, showing a mix of bullish and indifferent sentiments. The average 12-month price target is $88.67, an 8.13% increase from the previous average. The company demonstrates strong financial health with positive revenue growth of 8.41%, an excellent net margin of 8.48%, and efficient asset and equity utilization.

feed.title: Genco Shipping & Trading stock hits 52-week high at 23.94 USD
feed.summary: Genco Shipping & Trading Ltd's stock has reached a new 52-week high of $23.94 USD, reflecting a 67.77% increase over the past year and strong investor confidence. This surge occurs amidst a dispute where Genco rejected Diana Shipping Inc.'s unsolicited acquisition proposal of $20.60 per share, which Genco deemed undervalues the company. Genco's board is currently reviewing the proposal with advisors.

feed.title: AT&T Options Trading: A Deep Dive into Market Sentiment
feed.summary: Large investors have shown a mixed sentiment towards AT&T (NYSE: T) through recent options trades, with a split between bullish and bearish positions. Benzinga’s scanner identified 12 uncommon options trades, with a significant amount spent on both puts and calls targeting a price range of $22.0 to $30.0 over the next three months. The article also notes AT&T's current market standing, its primary business segments, and recent analyst ratings.

feed.title: Earnings call transcript: PPL Corporation’s Q4 2025 solid results despite revenue miss
feed.summary: PPL Corporation reported Q4 2025 EPS of $0.41, meeting expectations, but revenue of $2.27 billion missed forecasts by 5.42%. Despite the revenue miss, PPL's stock rose 1.9% in premarket trading. The company also announced an updated business plan, extending its 6%-8% annual EPS growth target through 2029, supported by $23 billion in capital investments focused on grid modernization and new generation.

feed.title: EVgo to Report Fourth Quarter and Full Year 2025 Results on March 3
feed.summary: EVgo Inc. announced it will release its fourth quarter and full year 2025 financial results on Tuesday, March 3, 2026. The financial results will be followed by a webcast hosted by EVgo management at 8 a.m. ET (5 a.m. PT). Details for accessing the webcast and where to find the press release and presentation materials are provided for investors.

feed.title: Phillips 66 SEC 10-K Report
feed.summary: Phillips 66 has released its 2025 10-K report, highlighting significant financial growth with a net income of $4,403 million, driven by asset sales and improved refining margins, despite an 8% decrease in sales revenue. The report details strategic initiatives focused on operational optimization, disciplined growth in Midstream and Chemicals, financial strength through debt reduction, and returning capital to shareholders. The company also outlines challenges including market volatility, regulatory changes, and operational risks, while emphasizing strategies to mitigate these through investments in renewable fuels and capital allocation.

feed.title: GEO Group And CoreCivic Stocks Are Getting Beaten Up Today – Why Such A Selloff?
feed.summary: Shares of GEO Group and CoreCivic are experiencing a significant selloff due to reports that Immigration and Customs Enforcement (ICE) plans to reduce its network of private detention facilities. This move would shift from a mixed model to a centralized system primarily using government-owned facilities, heavily impacting the revenue streams of these private prison companies. ICE contracts currently account for a substantial portion of both GEO Group's and CoreCivic's projected revenues.

feed.title: Yum China Holdings, Inc. (YUMC) Beats Estimates Despite Fierce Competition
feed.summary: Yum China Holdings, Inc. (YUMC) reported stronger-than-expected fourth-quarter and full-year results, with operating profit up 25% to $187 million and revenue up 8.8% to $2.8 billion, despite intense price competition in China's food delivery market. The company plans to expand its store count to over 25,000 and aims for an 11.5% operating margin by 2028, positioning itself as a leading restaurant company in China.

feed.title: PepsiCo Investor Sues to Circulate Animal Welfare Proxy Proposal
feed.summary: A PepsiCo shareholder has filed a lawsuit to compel the company to include an animal welfare proxy proposal in its proxy materials. The proposal, submitted through PETA, asks shareholders to vote for a report on suppliers' compliance with animal welfare policies and local laws. The shareholder claims PepsiCo Inc. is improperly planning to exclude the proposal.

feed.title: Centerpoint Energy stock reaches all-time high at 43.1 USD
feed.summary: CenterPoint Energy (CNP) stock has hit an all-time high of $43.09, demonstrating a 29% total return over the past year. Despite an InvestingPro analysis suggesting the stock is overvalued, the company maintains solid fundamentals, including 56 consecutive years of dividend payments and reaffirmed Q4 2025 earnings per share of $0.45, aligning with analyst expectations.

feed.title: Becton Dickinson stock hits 52-week high at 185.0 USD
feed.summary: Becton Dickinson (BDX) stock has reached a 52-week high of $184.95, reflecting strong investor confidence and a 19.6% year-to-date gain. The medical technology company, valued at $52.4 billion, recently outperformed fiscal Q1 2026 revenue and EPS expectations and announced a $1.6 billion debt repurchase. While analyst price targets have been adjusted, InvestingPro analysis suggests the stock is undervalued, supported by 55 consecutive years of dividend increases.

feed.title: EuroDry Posts Strong Q4 2025 Turnaround Driven by Higher Charter Rates
feed.summary: EuroDry Ltd. reported a strong financial turnaround in Q4 2025 with net revenues of $17.4 million, a 19.9% increase year-over-year, and net income of $3.2 million, or $1.14 per diluted share, significantly improving from a prior-year loss. This performance was driven by higher time charter equivalent rates averaging $16,262 per day and disciplined fleet utilization. The company also repurchased $5.3 million in shares and extended its buyback program, indicating confidence in its long-term outlook amidst a recovering drybulk shipping market.

feed.title: VICI Properties Inc: The REIT Wall Street Can’t Stop Watching
feed.summary: VICI Properties Inc. is a net-lease REIT specializing in entertainment and gaming properties like those on the Las Vegas Strip, collecting rent from long-term leases with built-in escalators. This model provides steady cash flow and attractive dividends, making it a watched income play for US investors seeking yield and real estate exposure without direct casino risk. While offering defensive qualities against rate volatility and a growing experiential portfolio, investors should consider its interest-rate sensitivity and concentration in the gaming sector.

feed.title: Kosmos Energy Sees Unusually Large Options Volume (NYSE:KOS)
feed.summary: Kosmos Energy (NYSE:KOS) experienced unusually large options trading, with call option volume increasing by 282% to 19,453 contracts. This surge in trading coincides with recent insider share sales by CFO Nealesh D. Shah and CAO Ronald W. Glass, totaling 272,844 shares worth $377,592 over the last 90 days. Despite the insider selling, institutional investors have been actively trading KOS shares, and the company's stock jumped 18.4% to $2.10 on Friday with heavy trading volume.

feed.title: Youdao (NYSE:DAO) Trading 7.9% Higher - Should You Buy?
feed.summary: Youdao (NYSE:DAO) saw its stock price jump 7.9% intraday on Friday, although trading volume was significantly lower than average. This surge comes despite mixed analyst ratings, with Citigroup upgrading the stock to "Buy" while Weiss Ratings maintained a "Sell" rating. The company recently beat earnings estimates but still faces challenges with a high P/E ratio and negative return on equity.

feed.title: Should You Buy Cenovus Stock at a Premium or Step Away Now?
feed.summary: Cenovus Energy Inc. (CVE) is near its 52-week high, driven by strong operational performance and consistent earnings, outperforming its peers in the Canadian energy sector. However, the article suggests that the stock is now overpriced, with its current valuation reflecting much of the recent optimism and leaving limited room for further growth. Due to high sensitivity to oil price fluctuations and a forecast of softer oil prices, the risk/reward balance appears skewed to the downside, leading to a "Strong Sell" recommendation.

feed.title: MCW TAKE PRIVATE ANNOUNCEMENT: Mister Car Wash, Inc. Shareholders are Notified of BFA Law’s Investigation into LGP’s Announced $7.00 per share Take Private Transaction
feed.summary: Bleichmar Fonti & Auld LLP (BFA Law) has launched an investigation into the take-private transaction of Mister Car Wash, Inc. (NASDAQ: MCW) by its controlling stockholder, Leonard Green & Partners, L.P. (LGP), for $7.00 per share. The investigation centers on potential breaches of fiduciary duties by Mister Car Wash's board and LGP, as LGP, owning over 66% of the company, can approve the sale with its own votes, raising concerns that the price might be unfairly low for public stockholders. Shareholders are encouraged to contact BFA Law if they believe they have legal options.

feed.title: Deadline Alert: PayPal Holdings, Inc. (PYPL) Shareholders Who Lost Money Urged To Contact Glancy Prongay Wolke & Rotter LLP About Securities Fraud Lawsuit
feed.summary: Glancy Prongay Wolke & Rotter LLP is urging PayPal Holdings, Inc. (PYPL) shareholders who suffered losses between February 25, 2025, and February 2, 2026, to contact them regarding a securities fraud lawsuit. The lawsuit alleges that PayPal made misleading statements and failed to disclose issues with its business execution, particularly in branded checkout, leading to a significant stock price drop after a leadership change and disappointing earnings report. Investors have until April 20, 2026, to file a lead plaintiff motion.

feed.title: Enbridge Shares Up 22.9% in a Year: Should You Buy the Stock or Wait?
feed.summary: Enbridge Inc. (ENB) shares have risen 22.9% over the past year, outperforming peers, and are approaching their 52-week high. The company boasts a stable business model with 98% of its EBITDA backed by long-term "take-or-pay" contracts, a 31-year dividend growth streak, and a significant project backlog. However, with a trailing 12-month EV/EBITDA of 16.48X compared to an industry average of 14.84X, the stock appears overvalued, suggesting investors might do well to wait for a better entry point, though existing holders could retain shares.

feed.title: KKR explores $1.5 billion sale of BMC Helix, sources say
feed.summary: Private equity firm KKR is actively pursuing the sale of its information services provider, BMC Helix, for an estimated $1.5 billion. This move comes as the software sector faces dealmaking slowdowns due to concerns about AI's impact on valuations. KKR also plans an IPO for BMC Software in 2026, subsequent to the Helix sale.

feed.title: Why F&G Annuities & Life (FG) Stock Is Falling Today
feed.summary: Shares of F&G Annuities & Life (NYSE:FG) fell 9.6% after the company reported weaker-than-expected fourth-quarter earnings and a significant drop in profitability. The company missed analyst expectations for adjusted EPS and book value per share, while its pre-tax profit margin also declined. Despite revenue beating estimates, investors focused on the weaker bottom-line figures, causing the stock to drop.

feed.title: International Flavors & Fragrances Inc.’s (IFF) Q4 Results Ease Concerns About European Slowdown, Says BofA
feed.summary: Bank of America raised its price target for International Flavors & Fragrances Inc. (IFF) to $100 from $88, maintaining a Buy rating, following IFF's Q4 and full-year 2025 results. BofA noted that IFF's Q4 performance and 2026 projections alleviated worries about a European slowdown, attributing the success to a two-year strategic shift towards targeted investments and organic development. The company reported Q4 net sales of $2.59 billion and adjusted EPS of $0.80, alongside full-year net sales of $10.89 billion, with positive outlooks for 2026 in sales and adjusted EBITDA.

feed.title: Analysts See Signs of Commodity Bottoming After Eastman Chemical Company (EMN) Q4 Results
feed.summary: Following Eastman Chemical Company's (EMN) Q4 2025 results, analysts are cautiously optimistic that commodity prices may have bottomed. Evercore ISI and RBC Capital raised their price targets for EMN, though RBC noted that a full recovery could be slow due to the intermediate nature of many of EMN's products. The company reported strong operational cash flow of approximately $1 billion and exceeded cost reduction goals, while also increasing its dividend for the sixteenth consecutive year.

feed.title: PPG Industries, Inc. (PPG) Gains Multiple PT Hikes Following Strong Market Reception to Earnings
feed.summary: PPG Industries, Inc. (PPG) has received multiple price target hikes from analysts like Evercore ISI, Mizhou, and Bernstein, following a strong market reception to its Q4 results despite an EPS miss. The company, a global supplier of paints and coatings, recently unveiled its 2026 global automotive color report, "Parallels," featuring the "Secret Safari" color of the year and highlighting strategic collaborations in China. Analysts continue to view PPG favorably, though the article suggests certain AI stocks might offer greater return potential.

feed.title: S&P Revises Energizer Outlook To Negative On Leverage Concerns By Investing.com
feed.summary: S&P Global Ratings has revised its outlook on Energizer Holdings Inc. from stable to negative due to concerns over the company's leverage, which is projected to reach approximately 6.3x in 2026, exceeding S&P's downgrade threshold. The revision follows weak first-quarter fiscal 2026 performance and a forecast incorporating continued organic volume declines across key segments. S&P highlighted risks from weak consumer spending, input cost volatility, and significant tariff and restructuring costs, while noting the company's efforts to sell down inventory and prepay debt.

feed.title: Churchill Says HISA’s Actions Represent ‘Troubling Pattern Of Overreach’
feed.summary: Churchill Downs, Inc. (CDI) has responded to a formal complaint from the Horseracing Integrity and Safety Authority (HISA) which accused CDI of failing to pay $5 million in required 2025 fees. CDI argues that HISA's actions represent a "troubling pattern of overreach" and mischaracterization of their commitment to safety, adding that they received HISA's letter minutes before an article about the complaint was published. HISA, however, claims CDI has been "freeloading" on their services, leading to a hearing scheduled for March 11.

feed.title: Essex Property Trust, Inc. (NYSE:ESS) Plans Dividend Increase - $2.59 Per Share
feed.summary: Essex Property Trust, Inc. (NYSE:ESS) has announced a quarterly dividend increase to $2.59 per share, up 0.8% from its previous dividend. While the current payout ratio of 170.5% indicates reliance on its balance sheet, future earnings guidance for FY2026 suggests the dividend could be sustainable with an anticipated payout ratio of approximately 62%. The company has a consistent history of dividend increases over the last 31 years.

feed.title: Robert W. Baird Lowers iRhythm Technologies (NASDAQ:IRTC) Price Target to $200.00
feed.summary: Robert W. Baird has lowered its price target for iRhythm Technologies (NASDAQ:IRTC) from $220.00 to $200.00, while maintaining an "outperform" rating, suggesting a potential upside of 36.14%. Despite the price target cut, and a 7.5% share drop, iRhythm exceeded quarterly earnings expectations with an EPS of $0.29 (vs. $0.02 expected) and revenue of $208.9M, a 27.1% year-over-year increase. The company remains unprofitable with a negative net margin and P/E ratio, and recent insider sales have occurred.

feed.title: COPT Defense Properties (NYSE:CDP) Announces Dividend Increase - $0.32 Per Share
feed.summary: COPT Defense Properties (NYSE:CDP) announced a quarterly dividend increase to $0.32 per share, up 4.9% from its previous dividend, resulting in an annualized yield of about 4.0%. The company recently surpassed Q4 earnings expectations with $0.70 EPS on $197.36 million revenue and provided optimistic FY2026 EPS guidance. Shares are currently trading near their 1-year high, reflecting strong financial health.

feed.title: Raymond James Financial, Inc. (NYSE:RJF) Plans Quarterly Dividend of $0.54
feed.summary: Raymond James Financial (NYSE:RJF) has declared a quarterly dividend of $0.54 per share, translating to an annualized $2.16 and a yield of approximately 1.4%. The company has consistently raised its dividend for the past three years, with a comfortable payout ratio of 19.5% which is expected to decrease to 17.9% next year based on analyst EPS forecasts. Despite slightly missing revenue estimates, the firm recently exceeded EPS expectations, reinforcing its "Hold" consensus rating and an average price target of $182.17 among analysts.

feed.title: Prothena Q4 Earnings Meet Estimates, Pipeline Progress in Focus
feed.summary: Prothena (PRTA) reported a Q4 2025 adjusted loss per share of 45 cents, meeting estimates, but revenues of $0.02 million missed the Zacks Consensus Estimate. Despite a 42% decline in share price over the past year, the company presented a positive outlook for 2026, expecting up to $105 million in clinical milestone payments from collaborations with Bristol Myers Squibb and Novo Nordisk. Prothena's pipeline continues to advance with key programs for Parkinson's disease, ATTR amyloidosis, and Alzheimer's disease progressing through late-stage trials.
